title: The Swarm plugin mirrors the Switchboard duplication pattern, swapping the remote layer
content:
Our Swarm plugin follows the same duplication pattern as the native Switchboard sync, with Swarm substituted for PostgreSQL:

```
CONNECT (browser)                    SWARM NETWORK
PGlite (local)                       /bytes (encrypted ops)
  - operations                         - operation batches (SAME ops)
  - documents                        Feeds (pointers)
  - relationships                      - doc/drive/user manifests
```

**Same 2x duplication pattern** — operations exist in PGlite AND on Swarm. The structural contract is identical:
- Local primary (PGlite) for hot reads
- Durable remote (Swarm instead of PostgreSQL) for recovery and cross-device

What changes:
- **Access control**: API key/auth token -> postage stamp + encryption
- **Operator**: server-operator-controlled -> decentralized, no single operator
- **Trust model**: trusted server -> self-sovereign (wallet-based)
- **Durability model**: row-deletable -> immutable content-addressed chunks

This is why the Swarm plugin slots into the reactor architecture cleanly — it preserves the same local+remote shape the SyncManager already expects, just routes traffic through a different channel.
description: Connect + Swarm plugin has the same 2x duplication as Connect + Switchboard — operations in PGlite and on Swarm. Swarm replaces PostgreSQL as the durable remote.
